title: Migrate CI to GitHub Actions and stop CircleCI checkout failures
description: Main is red because CircleCI fails before build/test in the checkout step: its SSH clone has no key. Move the repository-owned CI to GitHub Actions for build/test verification, and keep the repository config from continuing to fail in CircleCI during the transition.
acceptance_criteria: A GitHub Actions workflow in the repo runs the build and test quality gates on push and pull requests. The README CI badge points at the active workflow. Repository config no longer fails during CircleCI checkout for normal pushes, either because CircleCI is made non-failing or because the in-repo configuration is updated appropriately for the transition. Remote CI is verified after push, not just locally.

CircleCI still reports on the branch, but no longer fails at checkout: commit status context  succeeded and points to https://circleci.com/gh/jonbaldie/avg/17. Operational effect of the CircleCI config change: CircleCI remains green with a no-op transition job while repository-owned build/test enforcement moves to GitHub Actions.
Manager verified the original red main failure remotely before fixing it: CircleCI build 15 failed in Checkout code with git@github.com publickey permission denied. Implemented GitHub Actions in-repo, switched the README badge and CI wording to GitHub Actions, changed CircleCI to a temporary no-op transition job, updated test_cli.sh to avoid redundant rebuild races, and then pushed main. Verified commit 42ede72 on main after push: GitHub Actions run 25491375161 succeeded and CircleCI transition build 19 succeeded. Branch protection is not configured on main, so no required-check migration was needed.

title: Harden CLI input validation and child command failure handling
description: The avg CLI currently crashes on missing or malformed arguments and can print an average even when the measured command fails. Tighten argument parsing, reject invalid iteration counts, and make child command failures explicit so the core tool is trustworthy.
acceptance_criteria: Running with missing args or malformed iteration input exits cleanly with a helpful error. Zero and negative iteration counts are rejected. A non-zero exit from the measured command causes avg itself to fail with a clear message instead of silently printing an average. The implementation is developed with red/green TDD and no mocks, includes at least one automated regression check, and includes a manual execution note answering 'But does it actually work?'
notes:
Completion summary:
- Implemented hardened CLI parsing in `Main.hs` so missing args now emit `Usage: avg "<command>" <iterations>`, malformed iteration input reports the bad value, and zero/negative iteration counts are rejected before execution.
- Child command handling now stops on the first non-zero exit and `avg` exits non-zero with `Command failed with exit code <n>` instead of printing a misleading average.
- Red/green TDD used a real executable regression script (`test_cli.sh`) with no mocks. First red failure was the missing-args crash; green run passed after the hardening change.
- Manual execution also verified the bad-input and child-failure cases plus a successful `true` run. But does it actually work? Yes in this worktree: the CLI now fails clearly on invalid input and child errors, and succeeds cleanly on valid input.

title: Prepare worktree scaffold and delegate initial worker beads
description: Set up the repository for isolated worker worktrees, ensure .worktrees is present and ignored, and launch short-lived worker sessions on concrete beads with merge-ready instructions.
acceptance_criteria: .worktrees/ exists in the repo root and is gitignored. At least two child beads are created or updated for delegation. Worker instructions cite specific bead IDs, require bd prime on startup, require red/green TDD without mocks, require manual execution, and require detailed bead notes.
notes:
Manager coordination task. Ask repeatedly: But does it actually work?
Completed manager setup and delegation: created and gitignored .worktrees/, created focused child beads, launched isolated workers for avg-y77.2 and avg-y77.3, reviewed both branches skeptically, integrated the accepted changes with follow-up beads avg-y77.5 and avg-y77.6, and removed the worker worktrees with bd worktree remove after review.

title: Baseline reliability tranche for avg CLI
description: Create a small, well-defined tranche of work to harden the avg CLI and its delivery workflow. This epic groups short-lived tasks that can be delegated safely to isolated worker agents.
acceptance_criteria: Focused child issues exist for CLI validation, CI reliability, and any immediate follow-up discovered during execution. Coordination notes capture merge decisions and follow-up dependencies.
notes:
Manager-owned epic for parallel delegation and merge review.
Merge review outcome: avg-y77.2 was accepted functionally and then polished with avg-y77.6 to send diagnostics to stderr. avg-y77.3 provided the CI/test scaffold, but the initial happy-path-only test was strengthened during integration under avg-y77.5 so the shared make test target now runs the real CLI regression script. avg-y77.4 remains open for README alignment.
Epic complete: worker-delivered CLI hardening, CI regression coverage, stderr diagnostics, and README alignment are merged or integrated on main. Follow-up bug avg-9ow was captured during docs verification for the flaky repeated rebuild path.

title: Document local GHC 9.12.1 instability and validated build guidance
description: avg-2of showed intermittent build failures on macOS with GHC 9.12.1 in both the main checkout and isolated worktrees, with no safe repository-level mitigation validated yet. Capture the practical repo response in docs so local build guidance stays honest and repeatable: note the CI-validated GHC version, call out the observed macOS/GHC 9.12.1 instability, and direct contributors toward the validated verification workflow.
acceptance_criteria: 1. README and any relevant contributor-facing docs state the CI-validated GHC version and avoid implying that any arbitrary local GHC version is known-good. 2. The docs mention the observed macOS GHC 9.12.1 instability clearly enough to set expectations without overstating root cause. 3. The recommended local verification flow includes the concrete build/test/manual commands that were validated in this repo. 4. The doc update is itself validated against the current repo behavior and leaves no conflicting CI/toolchain guidance.
notes:
README wording choices:
- Stated GitHub Actions validates `ghc 9.4.3` because `.github/workflows/ci.yml` pins that exact version.
- Avoided claiming any arbitrary local GHC version is known-good; phrased local guidance as the only CI-validated compiler version today.
- Described macOS + `ghc 9.12.1` as having shown intermittent rebuild failures during repeated builds/tests in the same worktree, without asserting a proven root cause.
- Pointed readers to a validated local verification flow that keeps the manual `./build/avg "true" 2` smoke test and `./test_cli.sh` on the same built binary.

Commands validated against repo docs/CI:
- `bd prime`
- `bd show avg-68v`
- `bd update avg-68v --claim`
- `ghc --version` -> `The Glorious Glasgow Haskell Compilation System, version 9.12.1`
- Read `.github/workflows/ci.yml` -> CI pins `ghc-version: '9.4.3'` and runs `make build`, `make test`, `make clean`.
- `make build` -> PASS once (`[2 of 2] Linking build/avg`)
- `make test` immediately after prior build -> FAIL with `<no location info>: error: renameFile:renamePath:rename 'Main.o.tmp' to 'Main.o': does not exist`
- `make clean` -> PASS
- `make clean && make build && chmod +x test_cli.sh && ./test_cli.sh && make clean` -> PASS
- `make clean && make build && ./build/avg "true" 2 && make clean` -> FAIL on this attempt during `make build` with `<command line>: error: directory portion of 'build/avg' does not exist (used with "-o" option.)`
- `make clean && make build && ./build/avg "true" 2 && ./test_cli.sh && make clean` -> FAIL on this attempt during `make build` with rename `Main.o.tmp` error
- `make clean && make test && make clean` -> PASS because `test_cli.sh` builds once and reuses `./build/avg`

But does it actually work?
- The documented local verification flow is honest about the observed instability and points to the command sequence validated in-repo.
- The CI-aligned path (`make test`) still passes locally in this worktree when run from clean state.
- Repeated clean/build/manual flows remain intermittently flaky on macOS with local `ghc 9.12.1`, which is why the doc now treats that combination as unstable rather than supported.

Follow-up beads:
- No new bead created here because existing blocker/follow-up `avg-9ow` already tracks investigation of the flaky repeated rebuild path.
Manager integration: accepted the worker's README guidance with tightened wording. The shared branch now documents ghc 9.4.3 as the CI-validated compiler, notes the observed macOS ghc 9.12.1 instability conservatively, and adds a single-build local verification sequence. Revalidated in the main checkout with make clean && make build && ./build/avg "true" 2 && ./test_cli.sh && make clean && make test && make clean.

title: Diagnose GHC 9.12.1 single-build failure in worktrees
description: avg-9ow established that the repeated rebuild failure in this worktree is not only a parallel make/test race. On macOS with GHC 9.12.1, even a single clean build such as make clean && make build can intermittently fail with renameFile errors on Main.o.tmp or linker/otool failures for build/avg. Investigate whether this is a local toolchain regression, worktree/filesystem interaction, or an invocation issue, and identify a safe repository-level mitigation only if it is reproducible and validated. Discovered from avg-9ow while attempting the smallest root-cause fix for flaky repeated rebuilds.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Determine whether the intermittent GHC 9.12.1 build failure reproduces in the main checkout, worktrees, or both. 2. Capture a minimal reproducible command sequence and the observed errors in bead notes. 3. If a repository-level mitigation exists, implement it and verify with repeated clean builds, make test, and at least one manual ./build/avg run. 4. If no safe repo fix exists, document the environmental/toolchain conclusion and why the repo should not change.
notes:
Tested startup sequence and claimed issue. Evidence so far:
- Main checkout and worktree both reproduce intermittent failures; not worktree-specific.
- Direct clean builds can fail in both locations with GHC 9.12.1: renameFile:renamePath:rename Main.o.tmp to Main.o does not exist, clang: no such file or directory Main.o, and otool-classic: cant open file build/avg.
- Reproduced in worktree with direct command: /bin/rm -rf build && /bin/rm -f Main.hi Main.o && /bin/mkdir -p build && ghc -v0 -O2 Main.hs -o build/avg. Reproduced in root checkout with same command and with make build.
- make is /usr/bin/make (GNU Make 3.81). No make-related env vars set. Clearing MAKEFLAGS improves reliability for make build, but direct ghc still fails, so make is not the root cause.
- Compile-only commands are stable: ghc -fno-code Main.hs and ghc -O2 -no-link Main.hs passed repeatedly.
- Failures happen during object finalization or link/post-link phases, suggesting local GHC 9.12.1 plus toolchain/filesystem behavior rather than repo logic.
- -outputdir build did not eliminate the issue in the worktree; it still hit otool failure. In root checkout it happened to pass across 8 runs, so it is not yet a safe repository-level mitigation.
- -j1 improved reliability in some loops but still failed in root checkout with otool missing build/avg, so it is also not a validated mitigation.
- -keep-tmp-files looks promising in limited trials: 8 direct root runs and 10 make build GHC=ghc -keep-tmp-files plus ./test_cli.sh runs in the worktree passed. Not yet changing repo until reliability is better established.
- Successful verification runs observed: make clean && make build && make test && ./build/avg true 2 passed in both worktree and root on some runs.
Next: decide whether -keep-tmp-files is truly safe and reproducible enough for a minimal repo mitigation, or leave repo unchanged and document as toolchain/environment issue.
close_reason: Investigation completed: failures reproduce in both main and worktree checkouts under local GHC 9.12.1/macOS, and no safe repo-level mitigation was validated. Follow-up avg-68v captures the immediate repository action.

title: Disable or disconnect avg CircleCI project
description: The avg repository still receives a live CircleCI commit status () on main and has an active GitHub webhook targeting https://circleci.com/hooks/github. Disable or disconnect the external CircleCI project/webhook first so removing .circleci/config.yml will not reintroduce red or pending statuses. Verify the CircleCI status disappears from new commits before resuming avg-0gh.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Disable or disconnect the CircleCI integration so new commits no longer receive the ci/circleci: transition status. 2. Verify the GitHub webhook targeting circleci.com is removed or inactive, or otherwise document the exact alternative disconnect mechanism used. 3. Record before/after validation commands in bead notes and unblock avg-0gh once the external prerequisite is satisfied.
notes:
2026-05-07 external CircleCI disconnect attempt and validation:
- Startup sequence completed in worktree `/Users/jonathanbaldie/Code/avg/.worktrees/avg-5ct`: ran `bd prime`, `bd show avg-5ct`, then `bd update avg-5ct --claim` before any change.
- Before change, ran `gh api repos/jonbaldie/avg/commits/main/status` and observed commit `40ee6949ffb73aaafbe583d0d3ec38fcf4624721` had context `ci/circleci: transition` with target `https://circleci.com/gh/jonbaldie/avg/20` and description `Your tests passed on CircleCI!`.
- Before change, ran `gh api repos/jonbaldie/avg/hooks` and observed repository webhook id `393862628` with `active: true`, `config.url: https://circleci.com/hooks/github`, events `[fork, public, pull_request, push, repository]`, last_response `200 OK`.
- Confirmed GitHub CLI auth with `gh auth status` using account `jonbaldie` and repo-capable token scopes.
- Inspected recent webhook traffic with `gh api repos/jonbaldie/avg/hooks/393862628/deliveries`; latest push deliveries were succeeding with `status_code: 200`, including delivery at `2026-05-07T10:54:01.844Z`.
- First disable attempt `gh api repos/jonbaldie/avg/hooks/393862628 -X PATCH -f active=false` failed with HTTP 422 because GitHub interpreted `false` as a string: `For 'properties/active', "false" is not a boolean.`
- Successful external change used JSON request body: `gh api repos/jonbaldie/avg/hooks/393862628 --method PATCH --input - <<'EOF'
{"active":false}
EOF`
- Immediate response from GitHub for that PATCH showed webhook id `393862628` with `active: false` and updated_at `2026-05-07T11:10:46Z`.
- After change, re-ran `gh api repos/jonbaldie/avg/hooks` and confirmed the only CircleCI webhook remains present but now reports `active: false`. This is the authoritative disconnect mechanism actually applied.
- After change, re-ran `gh api repos/jonbaldie/avg/commits/main/status`; existing main commit still shows the historic `ci/circleci: transition` status for sha `40ee6949ffb73aaafbe583d0d3ec38fcf4624721`. This is expected for an already-published status and does not by itself prove continued delivery.
- After change, re-ran `gh api repos/jonbaldie/avg/hooks/393862628/deliveries`; no newer deliveries appeared beyond the pre-disable timestamps listed above.
- But does it actually work? Partially verified. The GitHub-side CircleCI webhook is definitively inactive now, which should stop new push events from being sent to CircleCI. However, because this bead must not commit/push and no other new commit was generated during the session, I could not directly observe a post-change commit lacking the `ci/circleci: transition` status. Acceptance criterion 2 is satisfied; criterion 1 is strongly indicated by the inactive webhook but not directly witnessed on a fresh commit during this session.
- No repo files changed. No commit or push performed.
- Recommendation for follow-up verification: after the next real commit to `jonbaldie/avg`, run `gh api repos/jonbaldie/avg/commits/<new-sha>/status` and confirm the CircleCI context is absent.
close_reason: External CircleCI integration was disconnected by deactivating the GitHub webhook. Historical statuses remain on old SHAs, but avg-0gh can now proceed with repo cleanup and future commit-level verification.

title: Investigate flaky repeated ghc rebuilds in worktree
description:
Repeated local rebuilds in the same worktree sometimes fail with GHC rename errors like rename 'Main.o.tmp' to 'Main.o': does not exist. This showed up while validating the README docs because rm -rf build && mkdir -p build && ghc -O2 Main.hs -o build/avg
[2 of 2] Linking build/avg and chmod +x test_cli.sh && ./test_cli.sh
test_cli.sh: all checks passed can fail after a prior build, even though a forced serial rebuild succeeds. Investigate the root cause and make the documented build/test workflow reliably repeatable.
notes:
Session notes:\n- Startup sequence completed in the assigned worktree: # Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when a beads workspace is resolved

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. bd dolt pull            (pull beads updates from main)
[ ] 4. git commit -m "..."     (commit code changes)
```

**Note:** This is an ephemeral branch (no upstream). Code is merged to main locally, not pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: run `bd dolt pull` at session start
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads updates from Dolt remote
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)
- `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans)

### Quality Tools
- `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections
- `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate)
- `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions
- `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes
- `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create
- `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections

### Lifecycle & Hygiene
- `bd defer <id> --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date
- `bd supersede <id> --with=<new-id>` - Mark issue as superseded
- `bd close <id> --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing
- `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity
- `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies
- `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans)
- `bd human <id>` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss)

### Structured Workflows
- `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates
- `bd mol pour <name>` - Start structured workflow from formula

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
bd dolt pull                # Pull latest beads from main
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit your changes
# Merge to main when ready (local merge, not push)
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```, ◐ avg-9ow [BUG] · Investigate flaky repeated ghc rebuilds in worktree   [● P2 · IN_PROGRESS]
Owner: Jonathan Baldie · Assignee: Jonathan Baldie · Type: bug
Created: 2026-05-07 · Started: 2026-05-07 · Updated: 2026-05-07

DESCRIPTION
Repeated local rebuilds in the same worktree sometimes fail with GHC rename errors like rename 'Main.o.tmp' to 'Main.o': does not exist. This showed up while validating the README docs because rm -rf build && mkdir -p build && ghc -O2 Main.hs -o build/avg
[2 of 2] Linking build/avg and chmod +x test_cli.sh && ./test_cli.sh
test_cli.sh: all checks passed can fail after a prior build, even though a forced serial rebuild succeeds. Investigate the root cause and make the documented build/test workflow reliably repeatable.

DISCOVERED FROM
  ◊ ✓ avg-y77.4: Refresh README to match validated CLI, test, and install behavior ● P3, ✓ Updated issue: avg-9ow — Investigate flaky repeated ghc rebuilds in worktree.\n- Reproduction: running the issue-reported commands in parallel reproduced the original symptom immediately. [2 of 2] Linking build/avg failed with , while test_cli.sh: all checks passed still passed in the competing process.\n- Serial behavior was initially misleading: rm -rf build && rm -f Main.hi Main.o
rm -rf build && mkdir -p build && ghc -O2 Main.hs -o build/avg
[1 of 2] Compiling Main             ( Main.hs, Main.o )
[2 of 2] Linking build/avg passed twice, suggesting the overlap between two GHC builds was one trigger.\n- Deeper diagnosis found the failure is broader: later single-process runs such as rm -rf build && rm -f Main.hi Main.o
rm -rf build && mkdir -p build && ghc -O2 Main.hs -o build/avg
[1 of 2] Compiling Main             ( Main.hs, Main.o )
[2 of 2] Linking build/avg, , [1 of 2] Compiling Main             ( Main.hs, build/Main.o )
[2 of 2] Linking build/avg [Objects changed], and [1 of 2] Compiling Main             ( Main.hs, build/Main.o ) [Flags changed]
[2 of 2] Linking build/avg [Objects changed] intermittently failed in this worktree on macOS with GHC 9.12.1. Observed errors included rename failures on  and , missing  during link, and an  panic on .\n- Because even the single-build path is unstable, I did not keep any speculative Makefile/test-script changes. There is not yet a safe smallest root-cause repository fix proven by red/green TDD.\n- Manual CLI verification on a successful build did work: rm -rf build && rm -f Main.hi Main.o
[1 of 2] Compiling Main             ( Main.hs, build/Main.o )
[2 of 2] Linking build/avg
Average elapsed time: 0.007207666666s printed . But does it actually work? The CLI itself works when a build succeeds; the unresolved problem is build reliability in this environment.\n- Follow-up bead created immediately:  to isolate the GHC 9.12.1/worktree single-build instability and determine whether a repository-level mitigation is possible or whether this should be documented as an environment/toolchain issue.\n- No code changes left in the worktree beyond this bead metadata update.
Manager triage: blocked on avg-2of after exploratory worker found broader GHC/worktree instability and left no code changes. Merge rejected; recycle the exploratory worktree.

title: Strengthen make test to run CLI regression coverage in CI
description: The initial make test target only checks the happy path, so CI would still miss regressions in CLI validation and child-command failure handling. Update the test target to run the real CLI regression script once the hardening changes are merged.
acceptance_criteria: The repository has a single non-interactive test entry point used by CI. That test entry point covers missing args, malformed iteration input, non-positive iteration counts, child command failure, and a successful run using real command execution and no mocks. CircleCI runs that strengthened test target.
notes:
Created during manager review of avg-y77.3 before merge. This is an integration-quality follow-up, not a duplicate of the original test-target task.
Discovered during merge review: avg-y77.3 added the CI/test scaffold, but its test only covers the happy path. Manager is integrating the stronger CLI regression script into the shared make/CI entry point before accepting the tranche.
Manager integration plan: take the worker CLI regression script, make it the shared make test entry point for CI, verify the combined branch locally, and only then accept the worker changes.
Manager integrated the worker branches by making test_cli.sh the shared non-interactive test entry point, wiring make test to it, and keeping CircleCI on checkout plus build/test/clean gates. Verification: make clean && make build && make test && make clean.

title: Add non-interactive test target and run it in CircleCI
description: The project currently builds in CI but has no real test target. Add a non-interactive make-based test workflow and update CircleCI to validate the checked-out revision with explicit quality gates.
acceptance_criteria: The make test target exists and runs non-interactively. CircleCI uses checkout rather than manually cloning the repository. CI runs the project build plus the new test target. The work is developed with red/green TDD and no mocks, includes at least one manual execution note answering But does it actually work, and leaves detailed bead notes about exact commands run.
notes: Exact commands run: `make test` (red, failed: no test target); `make test` (failed after first implementation because Make escaped `$` incorrectly in the grep anchor); `make test`; `printf '2\\n4\\n6\\n' | make test`; `make build`; `make test`; `make clean`; `make clean && make build && make test && make clean && make build && printf 'echo hello\\n' | ./build/avg "sh" 1`. Manual execution result: the built binary executed `sh` with stdin `echo hello`, printed `hello`, then printed an average duration line. But does it actually work? Yes for the implemented scope: the new non-interactive make target passes locally and the manual CLI flow works. Noted caveat: running multiple `ghc` builds in parallel in the same worktree races on `Main.o`, so verification was rerun serially.
close_reason: Completed: added non-interactive make test target, updated CircleCI to checkout and run build/test quality gates, and verified the flow locally with red/green notes recorded.

title: Verify fresh SHA has no CircleCI status after cleanup
description: Once the avg-0gh cleanup changes are committed and pushed, verify the first fresh commit SHA no longer receives the historical CircleCI status context and still shows the expected GitHub Actions checks. This keeps the external-disconnect verification explicit instead of leaving it implicit in manager notes.
acceptance_criteria: 1. After avg-0gh lands on a pushed SHA, query that SHA's GitHub commit status and check runs. 2. Confirm there is no ci/circleci: transition status on the fresh SHA. 3. Record the exact verification commands and results in bead notes; if CircleCI still appears, create or reopen the appropriate blocker immediately.
notes: Created during manager integration after deactivating the CircleCI webhook and deleting the transition shim locally. This task is intentionally deferred until a fresh pushed SHA exists.

title: Retire CircleCI transition shim after external project disablement
description: The repository now uses GitHub Actions for real build/test gates. CircleCI has been reduced to a no-op transition job so main stays green, but the external CircleCI project is still active and reports a redundant status. Once CircleCI is disabled outside the repo, remove the shim config cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: The external CircleCI project is disabled or disconnected. The .circleci/config.yml transition file is removed or intentionally archived without leaving red statuses. Repository docs and badges still point only at the active GitHub Actions workflow.
notes:
Removed the CircleCI transition shim now that the GitHub webhook is inactive. Exact repo changes: deleted .circleci/config.yml and updated README.md line 86 to remove the temporary-shim language so docs describe only GitHub Actions as the active CI path. These changes are sufficient because grep over README.md, .github/**/*, and .circleci/**/* finds no remaining CircleCI references or .circleci/config.yml mentions in repo docs/config, while .github/workflows/ci.yml remains the only in-repo CI workflow.

Automation run:
- gh api repos/jonbaldie/avg/hooks
- gh api repos/jonbaldie/avg/hooks/393862628
- gh api repos/jonbaldie/avg/commits/main/status
- make build
- make test
- make build && ./build/avg "true" 2 && make clean

Results:
- Webhook id 393862628 targeting https://circleci.com/hooks/github is active:false before/after cleanup validation.
- Current main commit status still shows historical context ci/circleci: transition on sha 40ee6949ffb73aaafbe583d0d3ec38fcf4624721, confirming prior state but not a fresh post-cleanup SHA.
- make build succeeded.
- make test succeeded via test_cli.sh: all checks passed.
- Manual execution ./build/avg "true" 2 succeeded and printed an average duration line. But does it actually work? Yes for the implemented scope: the binary still runs locally after the CI doc/config cleanup, and the repo now documents only GitHub Actions.

Validation note: initial standalone ./build/avg "true" 2 attempt hung past the 120s tool timeout because make clean had run in parallel and removed ./build/avg during that command group; reran serially as make build && ./build/avg "true" 2 && make clean and it passed.

Remaining verification gap: because this task must not create a commit or push, there is no fresh remote SHA to prove that ci/circleci: transition no longer appears after deleting .circleci/config.yml. Operationally, the CircleCI GitHub webhook is already inactive, so a future pushed commit should be checked after manager review to confirm only GitHub Actions statuses appear.
Manager integration: accepted the worker cleanup into the shared branch by deleting .circleci/config.yml and removing the last README transition-shim sentence. Revalidated in the main checkout with gh api repos/jonbaldie/avg/hooks (CircleCI webhook 393862628 remains active:false), make clean && make build && ./build/avg "true" 2 && ./test_cli.sh && make clean && make test && make clean. Remaining gap: a future fresh pushed SHA should confirm that no new ci/circleci: transition status appears.

title: Send CLI usage and failure diagnostics to stderr
description: The new CLI hardening path exits non-zero correctly, but diagnostic messages still go to stdout. Route usage and failure messages to stderr so the tool behaves better in scripts and CI.
acceptance_criteria: Usage errors and child-command failure messages are written to stderr while successful average output remains on stdout. Existing regression coverage continues to pass using real command execution and no mocks.
notes:
Discovered during manager review of avg-y77.2. Small CLI polish task; not a blocker for the main hardening behavior.
Manager plans a minimal follow-up during integration: route usage and failure diagnostics to stderr while keeping successful timing output on stdout and preserving the new regression coverage.
Manager changed CLI diagnostics to stderr via hPutStrLn stderr while keeping average output on stdout. Verification included redirect checks confirming child-command failures produce stderr output with no stdout leakage.

title: Fix make build target freshness
description: The current Makefile uses a directory named  as the target, so make: `build' is up to date. can incorrectly report the target up to date after source changes. This forces tests to use  and risks stale binaries during manual verification. Update the build target structure so ordinary make: `build' is up to date. reliably rebuilds when inputs change.
notes: Discovered during avg-y77.2 red/green TDD. `make build` can skip recompilation after `Main.hs` changes because the target name collides with the output directory. Current workaround in regression tests is `make build --always-make`, but the underlying Makefile should be corrected in follow-up work.
close_reason: Resolved during manager integration by declaring the Makefile build target phony and cleaning build artifacts, so ordinary make build no longer relies on directory freshness.

title: Refresh README to match validated CLI, test, and install behavior
description: Update the README only after the CLI behavior and test workflow are concrete, so docs reflect reality instead of aspirations.
acceptance_criteria: README documents validated CLI usage, build/install steps, and quoting expectations. Documentation matches the implemented failure behavior and test workflow. Any stale roadmap items related to tests or installation are updated accurately.
notes:
Kickoff plan: run the current README commands and inspect the validated CLI/test/build workflow first, note every mismatch, then update README to match reality, rerun the documented commands manually, and leave final notes answering 'But does it actually work?' with exact commands and outcomes.
Manager review found one docs regression before merge: the README changed the Docker example to omit the explicit avg command, but the image uses CMD rather than ENTRYPOINT. Verified with docker run: Average elapsed time: 0.023654173s prints an average, while  does not run avg. Fixing the README before accepting the docs branch.
Manager accepted the README refresh after one integration fix: the Docker example must invoke avg explicitly because the published image uses CMD rather than ENTRYPOINT. Verified locally with docker run and the documented local build/test commands.
